what_it_does: This file is the top-level map of contents and orientation home for the Company repo/Obsidian vault. It describes the system architecture, module boundaries, live capability registry, governed self-modification flows, and the rules agents and operators use to navigate and change the codebase.
observations: It declares itself in YAML frontmatter as a living descriptive map with aliases, tags, and related notes.
It opens with ASCII and Mermaid diagrams showing canvas, runtime, fabric, store, mcp_face, nodes, voice, panels, contracts, and extensions.
It contains a module table linking each directory to a one-line purpose, a constitution alias, and governing contracts.
A live registry block lists current node-types, RHM verbs, modes, panels, and fabric models, marked as auto-maintained.

It details the Suite as the brain, the RHM conversational voice, the decision-to-implementation wire, the session supervisor, the agent-session registry, the capability registry, search-to-handle-to-act, and point-in-time session launch.
It explains self-modification through node-types, declarative panels, and arbitrary extensions, all gated, additive, and git-reversible.
It defines three reversible change streams: self-apply, self-build, and operator checkpoint, surfaced through a single audit ledger.
It states a path-of-least-resistance law: the registry is the source of truth, confabulation is failure, and missing info surfaces as a NEEDS question.
standouts: The map is a drift-check target: Suite.map_drift fails loud if registered node-types, RHM verbs, or subsystems are missing here.
The decision-to-implementation wire requires a git checkpoint before implementation and surfaces every build for operator review.
The session supervisor owns a Claude Code fleet with strict laws: single writer, wall-clock watchdog, concurrency cap, and localhost-only exposure.
Live capabilities and sessions are addressable via cap:// and session:// schemes resolved through cached registries.
Operator checkpoints are a first-class reversible stream alongside autonomous self-apply and self-build commits.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Uses Obsidian wiki links and callouts such as info and note blocks.
Embeds code blocks for architecture diagrams.
Marks an auto-maintained registry region with HTML-style start/end comments.
References constitutions by alias rather than filename.
Tags governance rules with contract and standard identifiers like C1-C8, S1-S7, F-FIX, R1, etc.
Mixes high-level map prose with deep subsystem implementation detail in one long file.
concerns: The file is very long and mixes orientation, architecture, and detailed subsystem design, which may make it hard to maintain as a single entry point.
Many governance tags and cross-references are not defined in this file, so readers must follow numerous external links.
The live registry is auto-maintained but the surrounding prose is hand-written, so drift is possible despite the drift-check.
Some passages are extremely dense and acronym-heavy, which may reduce accessibility for new readers.
notes: This is the vault homepage and architectural index: a dense, self-referential map that tells agents where modules live, what the system can do right now, and how autonomous changes are governed and reversed.
questions: Where are the constitution files physically located and what do they contain?
What is the exact schema of the live registry entries and how does the drift-check work?
How does an operator approve or reject a proposed self-modification in the UI or CLI?
What are the precise definitions of contracts C1-C8 and standards S1-S7 referenced throughout?
What is the exact code-path relationship between the MCP face and the RHM?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the first orientation an agent loads and a maintained map of the linked code-knowledge space. The file does that, but it also acts as a detailed design document and governance ledger, so its actual scope is broader than a pure map.
intention_signals: It tries to make the codebase navigable and governable for both human operators and autonomous agents by centralizing architecture, live capabilities, and change rules in one authoritative, machine-checkable map.
novelty: It treats the repo map as a runtime artifact rather than static documentation, combining an Obsidian vault map, auto-maintained live registry, drift-check acceptance test, reversible self-modification streams, and addressable capability and session registries.
contribution: This file contributes a single, self-aware orientation artifact that links modules to their constitutions, exposes the system's current capabilities, records the rules for autonomous change, and provides the drift-check target that keeps the map honest.
summary_for_embedding: MAP.md is the top-level map of contents for the Company repo, describing architecture, module constitutions, live capability registry, governed self-modification flows, and navigation rules for agents and operators.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to be the first loaded orientation surface and maintained source of truth about the system's structure, capabilities, and safe autonomous-change conventions.
